title: Personalizing Incremental Video Search with Hybrid Text and ID Embeddings
gist: Apple engineers describe a personalization system deployed for Apple TV search that fuses a multilingual text-embedding model and a collaborative-filtering ID embedding model into an XGBoost ranker, showing the largest ranking gains on ambiguous short-prefix queries and for users with long watch histories, and confirming the lift in a three-week online A/B test with measurable conversion and tap-through gains.

themes.theme: Hybrid embedding architecture for real-time search

insights.claim: Apple TV's incremental search personalization system combines two learned item embedding spaces at ranking time: a multilingual text encoder (TextEmb) fine-tuned on co-engagement triplets via contrastive learning, and an ID-based collaborative embedding model (IdEmb) trained on interaction-derived positives, fused via a pairwise XGBoost ranker using user-item cosine similarities.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A concrete, production-proven blueprint for combining semantic and collaborative signals in a low-latency (per-keystroke) ranking pipeline -- a reusable architecture pattern for any real-time personalized search product.
insights.quote: we construct user representations from recent watch history and inject text- and ID-based user–item cosine similarities into a pairwise XGBoost ranker

themes.theme: Personalization pays off most where intent is unclear

insights.claim: In offline evaluation with temporally held-out data, for sessions with user history the personalized ranker improved NDCG@10 by 2.99% and MRR by 3.30% over a non-personalized baseline.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Establishes the aggregate offline lift baseline before drilling into where value concentrates -- useful comparator for evaluating similar personalization ROI elsewhere.
insights.quote: the personalized ranker improves NDCG@10 by 2.99% and MRR by 3.30% over the non-personalized baseline

insights.claim: Personalization gains are far larger on ambiguous 1-3 character prefix queries (NDCG@10 lift of +8.63%) than on longer, more fully specified queries (+1.46% lift), showing that personalization matters most while user intent is still forming during incremental (per-keystroke) search.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Quantifies exactly where personalization ROI concentrates in incremental search UX -- a design signal for any product with type-ahead or per-keystroke search to prioritize personalization investment on short/ambiguous queries.
insights.quote: on ambiguous prefix queries (1–3 characters), NDCG@10 lift is +8.63%, versus only +1.46% on longer, more fully specified queries
insights.locator: Abstract, slice analysis on query length

insights.claim: Users with longer watch histories benefit disproportionately more from personalization: NDCG lift rises from +2.13% for users with 1-5 history items to +4.37% for users with 51-100 items, even though the non-personalized baseline NDCG@10 is actually lower for long-history users (0.733 to 0.680), indicating personalization adds the most value exactly where default ranking underperforms.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Shows a compounding-data-moat dynamic: users who generate more behavioral data get a disproportionately better product experience, reinforcing retention for high-engagement users on Apple's platform.
insights.quote: This larger lift occurs even though baseline relevance is lower for long-history cohorts (NDCG@10 drops from 0.733 to 0.680), indicating that personalization adds the most value where default ranking underperforms.

insights.claim: In a three-week online controlled experiment, the personalized ranking system produced statistically significant gains of +1.14% tap-through rate and +1.23% conversion rate, along with a 2.91% improvement in the rank position of the item ultimately selected/converted.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Translates the offline ranking-metric gains into confirmed real-world engagement and conversion lift on a live Apple product, evidence the technique has measurable business value, not just academic benchmark improvement.
insights.quote: treatment yields statistically significant gains of +1.14% tap-through rate and +1.23% conversion rate, with a 2.91% improvement in converted-item rank position

insights.claim: The team evaluates embedding quality using LLM-judged similarity labels on a held-out corpus specifically to reduce click/exposure bias that would otherwise contaminate standard click-based evaluation.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A methodological practice worth emulating: using LLM-as-judge evaluation to de-bias offline embedding-quality assessment from the exposure bias inherent in historical click logs.
insights.quote: evaluate embedding quality on a held-out corpus with LLM-judged similarity labels to reduce click/exposure bias
